# 'downstack merge' retargets the immediate upstack after deleting
# the merged branch, but leaves replay for an explicit restack.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git config spice.experiment.merge true
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create a stack: main -> feat1 -> feat2 -> feat3
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m 'Add feat1'
git add feat2.txt
gs bc feat2 -m 'Add feat2'
git add feat3.txt
gs bc feat3 -m 'Add feat3'

# submit the stack
gs downstack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'
stderr 'Created #3'

# merge only feat1
gs bco feat1
env ROBOT_INPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.golden ROBOT_OUTPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.actual
gs downstack merge
cmp $WORK/robot-merge.actual $WORK/robot-merge.golden
stderr 'feat1: merging #1:'
stderr 'feat2: retargeted upstack onto main'
stderr 'feat1: #1 was merged'
stderr 'feat1: deleted'

# verify all files are present on feat3
gs bco feat3
exec cat feat1.txt
stdout 'feature 1'
exec cat feat2.txt
stdout 'feature 2'
exec cat feat3.txt
stdout 'feature 3'

# The immediate upstack is retargeted during deletion,
# but replay is left for an explicit restack.
gs branch restack --branch feat2
stderr 'feat2: restacked on main'

# Descendants are left for an explicit restack.
gs branch restack --branch feat3
stderr 'feat3: restacked on feat2'
gs branch restack --branch feat3
stderr 'does not need to be restacked'

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feature 1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feature 2
-- repo/feat3.txt --
feature 3
-- robot-merge.golden --
===
> Merge 1 change(s) bottom-up?: [Y/n]
>   feat1 (#1)
true
